data.title: Capstone stock rises on cost rationalization program
data.description:
Capstone Holding (CAPS) said it has launched a cost-cutting program aimed at reaching positive free cash flow and EBITDA.

The move will eliminate about $2M in annual corporate overhead costs, effective immediately. The company has eliminated non-core investor relations and consulting expenditures, resulting in an immediate ~$1.7M reduction in operating expenses, directly accretive to the bottom line.

"2026 is the year we translate the $70 million run-rate platform we've built into a durable profitability engine," said CEO Matthew Lipman. "By reducing corporate overhead by $2.0 million, we are immediately positioning the Company for sustainable, cash-generative growth. We have a clear line of sight to a positive EBITDA run-rate."

In addition, Lipman has voluntarily reduced his annual base cash salary to $1.00 for the next year, aligning executive remuneration directly with common shareholder equity appreciation.

The cost cuts are expected to start showing results in the first quarter, with the full impact clear by the quarter ending June 30, 2026. Management expects the savings to deliver a positive corporate EBITDA run rate starting in Q2 2026, independent of revenue growth.

By the first week of February, the company expects to release an earnings power presentation outlining a range of EBITDA expectations under various macroeconomic assumptions.

CAPS +15.8% premarket to $0.86

Source: Press Release

data.title: Smithfield Foods to acquire Nathan’s Famous for $450M; NATH shares jump 7% premarket
data.description:
• Smithfield Foods (SFD) and Nathan’s Famous (NATH) have entered into a definitive merger agreement for Smithfield Foods to acquire all of Nathan’s Famous’ issued and outstanding shares for $102.00 per share in cash, which represents an enterprise value of ~$450 million.
  • Smithfield Foods has held an exclusive license to manufacture, distribute, market, and sell Nathan’s Famous–branded hot dogs and related products across the U.S., Canada, and Sam’s Clubs in Mexico, serving both retail and foodservice channels since March 2014. The license is scheduled to expire in March 2032.
  • Completing the acquisition will secure Smithfield’s long-term rights to the Nathan’s Famous brand and support its growth in both retail and foodservice markets.
  • The transaction represents a valuation of approximately 12.4x Nathan’s Famous’s LTM adjusted EBITDA and a multiple of approximately 10.0x post-synergies, the companies said.
  • Smithfield Foods (SFD) expects to achieve annual cost synergies of approximately $9 million by the second anniversary of the deal closing.
  • Nathan's Famous (NATH) shares rose over 7% to $99.61 premarket.

data.title: Stock index futures tread water ahead of Trump's Davos speech
data.description:
Stock index futures were little changed Wednesday as investors focused on President Donald Trump’s upcoming speech at Davos, with markets watching closely for signals on which policy proposals officials might actually pursue.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) were flat. Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) fell 0.2% and Dow futures (INDU) edged lower by 0.1%.

U.S. Treasury yields fell across the curve after the rise in the previous session. The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) slipped to 4.28%, while the 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) slipped to 4.91%. On the shorter end, the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) fell to 3.59%.

Trump "often gives quite strong and unusually wide-ranging statements. However, investors’ focus may be less on Trump’s rhetoric and more on which parts of Trump’s remarks officials might actually seek to act upon,” said UBS chief economist Paul Donovan.

Trump is arriving a few hours late in Davos due to a minor electrical fault that caused Air Force One to turn back last night.

Top gainers in premarket trading included United Airlines Holdings (UAL) +3.2% and EQT (EQT), +2.8%. Decliners included Netflix (NFLX) -6.7%, The Kraft Heinz Company (KHC) -5.9%, and Revvity (RVTY) -2.57%.

data.title: Serve Robotics expands physical AI into hospitals with a key acquisition
data.description:
Serve Robotics Inc. (SERV) announced an agreement to acquire AI robotics company Diligent Robotics for an undisclosed amount.

Diligent Robotics is an AI robotics company known for its hospital assistant robots, marking Serve’s expansion into indoor and healthcare environments. Founded in 2017 by Andrea Thomaz and Vivian Chu, Diligent has raised over $100 million from investors including Tiger Global and Canaan and developed Moxi, which is a hospital delivery robot deployed in over 25 U.S. hospitals and responsible for more than 1.25 million autonomous deliveries. Moxi, powered by NVIDIA's (NVDA) AI technology, supports nurses and staff by automating routine tasks, improving efficiency, and improving patient care quality.

Under the merger terms, Serve Robotics (SERV) will issue $29 million in its common stock to Diligent shareholders, with an additional earn-out potential of up to $5.3 million contingent on performance milestones. The deal is expected to close in Q1, subject to customary conditions, including regulatory and Nasdaq listing approvals. 

The acquisition is expected to lead to Serve Robotics' (SERV) autonomous mobility platform being integrated with Diligent Robotics's manipulation and indoor navigation capabilities, creating a unified physical AI ecosystem that operates safely around people in dynamic environments. Serve Robotics (SERV) plans to leverage the merger to accelerate its AI learning flywheel, enhance fleet economics, and expand into other complex indoor sectors. Each hospital deployment of Moxi generates approximately $200,000–$400,000 annually, providing a high-revenue growth opportunity for the company.

Following the acquisition closing, Diligent Robotics will continue operating as a wholly owned subsidiary under Thomaz’s leadership.

"This acquisition accelerates Serve’s evolution from a robotic delivery company into a full-stack autonomy platform," stated Dr. Ali Kashani, CEO of Serve Robotics. "We’ve proven we can deploy robots safely and reliably at scale in complex urban environments. By extending our platform beyond sidewalks and into hospitals, we’re expanding where our Physical AI can operate, learn, and create value," added Kashani.

Shares of Serve Robotics (SERV) were up 3.7% in premarket trading on Wednesday.

data.title: Dime Community Bancshares Non-GAAP EPS of $0.79 beats by $0.08, revenue of $124M beats by $5.87M
data.description:
• Dime Community Bancshares press release (DCOM): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.79 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $124M (+24.6% Y/Y) beats by $5.87M.

  • The adjusted efficiency ratio decreased to 50.3% for the fourth quarter of 2025 compared to 53.1% for the prior quarter.

  • Adjusted Margin – Others increased by 45.5% Y/Y in 3Q26 to $27.5 million.

data.title: Elon Musk's most boring company is making a lot of noise in Nevada
data.description:
Elon Musk's lowest-profile company is still making a lot of noise. The Boring Company is working with the Economic Development Authority of Western Nevada on a conceptual plan for a tunnel running roughly nine miles under Interstate 80 from Reno to the Tesla (TSLA) Gigafactory at the Tahoe-Reno Industrial Center, funded by a $50,000 feasibility and conceptual design contract.

The study on the potential for a Boring tunnel is one of several options being considered by transportation officials to ease worsening congestion and accidents on I-80 near Reno, as employment at the Tahoe Reno Industrial Center has surged to about 22,000 workers, including thousands at Tesla (TSLA) and Panasonic.

The Boring Company is also active in Las Vegas, with the Vegas Loop experiencing significant expansion in early 2026 as the transportation system evolves from its original convention center focus into a comprehensive citywide transit network. The network currently has eight operational stations in the Strip corridor, including five at the Las Vegas Convention Center, plus stations at Resorts World (opened in 2022), Westgate (opened in January 2025), and Encore (opened in April 2025). The most anticipated development is the expanding airport connection to Harry Reid International Airport, scheduled to continue growing to a capacity to transport up to 20,000 people per hour.

A 2022 Series C funding round valued The Boring Company at $5.7 billion.

Caterpillar (CAT), AECOM (ACM), and Jacobs Engineering Group (J) are three companies that are very loosely related to Boring's unique main business. Elon Musk's other companies include Tesla (TSLA), SpaceX (SPACE), Neuralink, and xAI (XAI).
